reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle is simple (91 - 44 = 47), so I’m confident Q1-A is correct. All previous games have also used easy arithmetic, and trusting each player’s own capsule set (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) has always produced a correct password. Verifying would cost coins and still risks a no-volunteer round, which previously led to my random elimination in Game 1. By volunteering immediately with each player’s own capsule, I guarantee there is at least one volunteer this round, avoid the random-death scenario, spend no coins, and the chance another player mis-solved their basic arithmetic puzzle seems extremely low.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (33 + 27 = 60) is straightforward, and all other questions have so far been simple arithmetic with no past errors from any player. Verifying would cost coins and still leave a risk of random death if no one volunteers. Given only single, non-conflicting capsules exist for each question, volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D maximizes my chance to escape with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All past games used simple arithmetic with no wrong or conflicting capsules, so the chance any current capsule is wrong is extremely low. Verifying would waste a coin and still leave the possibility of random death if no one volunteers, and passing has that same random-death risk with no compensating benefit. I’m confident in my own answer to 56 ÷ 7 and have no evidence others are unreliable, so volunteering now with the single capsule for each question is the safest and most efficient option while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
